en.name: improving WEtland GOvernance through a COmmunity Of Practice
en.description:
Mediterranean Wetlands are extremely vulnerable ecosystems, often under environmental protection. The Ramsar Convention defines them as one of the most productive and biodiverse environments in the world, providing the habitat on which countless species depend. They are highly subject to external pressures including i) the state of health of the river basins to which they refer; ii) intensive agriculture causing water consumption and water and soil pollution; iii) heavy urbanization.
Wetlands are often challenged by the poor coordination and capacity of institutions to manage complex territorial dynamics needing different levels of planning. Thus, the active protection of wetlands biodiversity requires overcoming the current sectoral approach and addressing the problem systemically. 
WE GO COOP addresses these issues by promoting the implementation of the Wetland Contract, a multilevel collaborative governance tool tested in several EU-funded projects, fostering coordination between organizations previously involved and new central stakeholders.
The project will:
1- Set up a Community of Practice (CoP) amongst actors engaged in Wetland Contracts at the Mediterranean level, capitalizing on the outcomes of former EU-funded projects, sharing challenges and solutions;
2- Exchange experiences, methodologies, and strategies, and transfer the Wetland Contract tool to new contexts in the Euro-Med region (Croatia, Portugal, Albania) and the South and Middle East Med region.
Transnational cooperation becomes the tool through which WE GO COOP forms the CoP uniting different perspectives facing similar challenges, enabling the project to meet the needs of wetlands on a large scale, ensuring the transferability of its results.
WE GO COOP is expected to:
1- Improve knowledge about wetland collaborative governance processes,
2- Improve collaboration between researchers, policy-makers, experts, environmental associations, key stakeholders, and communities.
en.expected_achievements:
To set up a Mediterranean Community of Practice between organizations committed to the Wetland Contracts, to 
capitalize and transfer the outcomes of former EU-funded MED projects on the Wetland Contract tool and create new synergies.
To exchange knowledge, experience and practices on Wetland Contract tool application and transfer the methodology from PP participating in EU funded project on wetland governance, to new partners in new regions.

-Signed the Partnership Agreement and Subsidy Contract
-Prepared the Rules of Procedure of the Steering Committee
-Actively used Basecamp for monitoring deadlines and storing final deliverables
-Organized the Kick-Off meeting (online) on 29.2.2024  with all partners' participation

Regarding Communication and Dissemination, the Partnership:
-Finalized the Communication Strategy
-Created the project social media (LinkedIN, Facebook, Instagram)
-Created the project website according to the guidelines of the Programme

-Had an active presence in the social media

Regarding contribution to the Amplification Strategy, the Partnership:

Regarding the implementation of the Work Packages, a summary is presented bellow:

fr.name: Améliorer la gouvernance des zones humides par une communauté de pratique
fr.description:
Les zones humides méditerranéennes sont des écosystèmes extrêmement vulnérables, souvent sous protection environnementale. La Convention de Ramsar les définit comme l'un des environnements les plus productifs et les plus riches en biodiversité au monde. Ils sont fortement soumis à des pressions externes dont i)l'état de santé des bassins fluviaux auxquels ils se rapportent; ii)agriculture intensive entraînant consommation d'eau et pollution; iii)forte urbanisation. 
Les zones humides sont souvent confrontées à une mauvaise coordination et capacité des institutions à gérer d’une facon concertée et integrée les dynamiques territoriales complexes nécessitant de différents niveaux de planification. 
WE GO COOP répond à ces enjeux en promouvant la mise en œuvre du Contrat Zones Humides, un outil de gouvernance collaborative à plusieurs niveaux testé dans de projets financés par l'UE, favorisant la coordination entre les organisations impliquées et les nouvelles parties prenantes centrales. 
Le projet vise à: i)Mettre en place une Communauté de Pratique (CoP) entre les acteurs engagés dans les Contrats Zones Humides au niveau méditerranéen, en capitalisant sur les résultats des anciens projets financés par l'UE, en partageant les défis et les solutions; ii)Echanger des expériences, méthodologies et stratégies, et transférer l'outil Contrat Zones Humides à de nouveaux contextes dans la région Euro-Med (Croatie, Portugal, Albanie) et dans la région Sud et Moyen-Orient Med.
La coopération transnationale devient l'outil par lequel WE GO COOP forme la CoP réunissant différentes perspectives face à des défis similaires, répondant aux besoins des zones humides à grande échelle, assurant la transférabilité de ses résultats. 
WE GO COOP est censé: i)Améliorer les connaissances sur les processus de gouvernance collaborative des zones humides; ii)Améliorer la collaboration entre chercheurs, décideurs, associations environnementales, acteurs clés et communautés.

fr.name: MAking Sustainable Tourism possible
fr.description: Les défis communs abordés par le projet sont : (i) surmonter les différences et unir les PME d'hébergement méditerranéennes autour de l'objectif commun de remodeler le tourisme de manière plus durable ; (ii) les stimuler et les soutenir dans la mise en œuvre de systèmes de gestion durables, à travers le développement de solutions facilement applicables. L'objectif général est de faciliter et de soutenir les PME d'hébergement méditerranéennes dans la mise en œuvre d'un système de gestion durable, qui est actuellement un facteur critique pour le succès et la compétitivité à long terme. Le changement attendu est de faciliter la transition vers un tourisme durable par ces PME, en leur permettant de mettre en place un système de gestion durable et de préparer le terrain pour obtenir la certification ISO 21401:2018. À cette fin, le projet prévoit le développement de deux résultats : un protocole de durabilité et un outil d'auto-évaluation. Ce dernier permettra à ces PME de vérifier leur degré actuel de durabilité ; sur la base des résultats obtenus, le protocole indiquera les activités nécessaires pour se conformer à la norme. L'approche consistera à réaliser une étude basée sur l'ISO 21401:2018 et l'identification des barrières et des besoins des PME d'hébergement en termes de gestion durable. Compte tenu des différentes réalités socio-économiques et des étapes de développement touristique dans les cinq pays partenaires, la coopération transnationale est un facteur crucial pour garantir la réalisation de haute qualité des objectifs ainsi que la diffusion des résultats. Le projet représente une nouveauté par rapport aux pratiques actuelles dans le secteur de l'hébergement ; il propose une étude visant à définir des solutions qui équilibrent les spécificités de toutes les dimensions de la durabilité. Jusqu'à présent, la plupart des professionnels et universitaires du tourisme ont proposé la durabilité des installations d'hébergement en se basant presque exclusivement sur la dimension environnementale, entraînant un retard dans le plein développement d'une culture organisationnelle durable.
fr.expected_achievements:
Provide empirical support for the development of effective and efficient Sustainability protocol and Sustainability Self-Assessment tool.
Based on ISO 21401:2018 standard and sustainable tourism study in partner countries, it is intended to define a sustainability protocol that can operationally support SMEs accommodation in implementing and adopting a sustainable management system.
Based on tourism sector study and protocol, the aim is to set up a self-assessment tool that can support SMEs accommodation in verifying their sustainability current level and identifying the efforts to comply with a sustainable management system.
Strengthen involvement of accommodation SME establishments and other sector stakeholders to validate project outcomes through national workshops.
Prepare Policy recommendations for the adoption and exploitation of project results.

Disseminating results and raising awareness with an aim to activate an effective external stakeholders group. 
Creating a EU network of “Accommodation establishments for sustainable tourism”.

en.name: MAking Sustainable Tourism possible
en.description: The common challenges addressed by the project are: (i) overcoming differences and uniting Mediterranean SMEs accommodation around the common objective of reshaping tourism in more sustainable ways; (ii) stimulating and supporting them in the implementation of sustainable management systems, through the development of easily applicable solutions. The overall objective is to facilitate and support Mediterranean SMEs accommodation in implementing a sustainable management system, which is currently a critical factor for the success and competitiveness in the long term. The expected change is to facilitate the transition towards sustainable tourism by such SMEs, enabling them to implement a sustainable management system and to pave the way for achieving the ISO 21401:2018 certification. To this end, the project foresees the development of two outputs: sustainability protocol and the self-assessment tool. The latter will enable these SMEs to check their current degree of sustainability; based on the results obtained, the protocol will indicate the activities needed to comply with the standard. The approach will be to conduct a study based on ISO 21401:2018 and the identification of the barriers and needs of SMEs accomodation in terms of sustainable management. Given the different socio-economic realities and stages of tourism development in the five partner countries, transnational cooperation is a crucial factor in ensuring the high-quality achievement of the objectives as well as the dissemination of the results. The project represents a novelty compared to current practices in the accommodation sector; it proposes a study aimed at defining solutions that balance the specificities of all dimensions of sustainability. To date, most tourism professionals and scholars have proposed the sustainability of accommodation facilities based almost exclusively on the environmental dimension, causing a delay in the full development of a sustainable organisational culture.

en.actual_achievements:
The MAST project has made significant strides since its inception, demonstrating a robust commitment to advancing sustainable tourism practices in the Mediterranean region. The project's initial phase has been marked by noteworthy achievements that have set a solid foundation for future endeavours.

From the outset, the consortium, led by UNIURB, has shown exemplary coordination and management. The successful online kick-off meeting in March 2024 aligned all partners, establishing a clear vision for the project's objectives. This was further reinforced by the Steering Committee meeting held in Barcelona in May 2024, which provided a valuable platform for in-depth discussions and strategic planning.

A cornerstone of the project's early success has been the establishment of efficient communication and management structures. Implementing the Basecamp platform for internal project management, along with launching a WordPress website and active social media channels, has facilitated seamless collaboration amongst partners and effective engagement with external stakeholders.

The research component of the project has made impressive headway. A comprehensive methodology for investigating sustainability in the accommodation sector has been meticulously designed, encompassing scoping studies, quantitative analyses, and qualitative research. To date, 26 qualitative interviews have been conducted across partner countries, providing rich insights into the current landscape of sustainable practices in the Mediterranean tourism industry. A thorough literature review analysing approximately 45 relevant studies has also been undertaken, laying the groundwork for a robust theoretical framework.

In parallel with these research efforts, significant progress has been made in developing practical tools. The consortium has begun work on a Market Search Report and initiated the planning phase for a sustainability self-assessment tool. These endeavours have been informed by carefully studying existing sustainability labels and schemes in the tourism industry, ensuring that the project's outputs will be innovative and relevant to industry needs.

Stakeholder engagement has been a priority from the outset. The project has been represented at key events such as the Sustainable Tourism Community kick-off meeting in Rome and various EU MED meetings. These platforms have provided valuable opportunities for networking, knowledge sharing, and raising awareness about the MAST project's objectives and potential benefits.

Quality assurance has not been overlooked, with a comprehensive MAST Quality Assurance plan developed to guide all project activities. This attention to quality underpins the project's commitment to delivering high-calibre outputs that will have a lasting impact on the sector.

The collaborative nature of the MAST project has proven to be one of its greatest strengths. By bringing together diverse expertise from across the Mediterranean region, the consortium has developed a nuanced and comprehensive approach to sustainability in tourism. This multi-stakeholder, cross-border cooperation has facilitated valuable knowledge exchange on sustainability practices and challenges specific to different contexts within the Mediterranean.

In conclusion, the MAST project has made remarkable progress in its initial phase. The foundations laid through meticulous research, strategic planning, and stakeholder engagement have positioned the project to significantly contribute to sustainable tourism practices in the Mediterranean region. As the project moves forward, it is well-equipped to develop innovative tools and strategies to drive the transition towards more sustainable accommodation practices, ultimately contributing to Mediterranean tourism's long-term environmental, social, and economic sustainability.
The MAST project has made substantial progress in helping small and medium-sized accommodation providers across Europe adopt sustainable practices. By combining expertise from partners in Italy, Spain, Greece, Slovenia, and Bosnia and Herzegovina, the team has delivered valuable insights, tools, and outreach activities designed to address real-world challenges faced by the hospitality sector.

A major milestone is the completion of the Consultation Report on SME Needs and Barriers to Sustainability (D2.1), led by the University of Urbino. This report synthesises data from over 211 accommodation providers and 22 interviews, identifying common challenges in environmental, social, and economic sustainability. Partners such as CENFIM in Spain and Sarajevo Susret Kultura in Bosnia contributed regional survey data, while 3WIN in Greece shared examples of successful sustainability practices from local hotels. The collaborative review process ensured the final report offers practical, actionable recommendations tailored to SMEs.

Building on this research, the project is developing tools to support sustainable transitions. For example, 3WIN is creating an online self-assessment tool to help accommodation providers evaluate their sustainability performance. Market research and external expertise have informed its design, which aligns with the ISO 21401 standard. A beta version is expected by mid-2025. Meanwhile, the University of Primorska in Slovenia has produced guidelines for a Sustainability Protocol, translating research findings into clear steps for SMEs to follow.

Alongside these practical tools, the project has prioritised stakeholder engagement. Events like the Info Day in Sarajevo brought together over 150 participants to discuss sustainability challenges and solutions. 3WIN also showcased Greek hotel success stories at this event, inspiring peer learning. Upcoming activities include a Steering Committee Meeting in Greece in 2025, designed to further engage stakeholders. Communication efforts—such as social media campaigns, website updates, and a promotional video—have raised awareness of the project’s goals and early achievements.

Underpinning these efforts is strong coordination and quality assurance. The University of Urbino, as lead partner, has ensured smooth collaboration between work packages, including the transition from research (WP1) to tool development (WP3). Regular meetings, such as the Steering Committee in Sarajevo, have kept partners aligned. CENFIM’s rigorous quality checks, including surveys and progress reviews, have maintained high standards across deliverables.

The MAST project highlights the power of cross-border cooperation. Partners have pooled expertise in sustainability standards, data analysis, and tool development, while regional diversity—from Spanish hotels to Bosnian guesthouses—ensures solutions are adaptable to different contexts. Early milestones, such as the accelerated completion of the Market Search Report, demonstrate the efficiency of this collaborative approach.

Looking ahead, the team will focus on finalising the self-assessment tool and rolling out training webinars to empower SMEs. By combining research, innovation, and partnership, MAST is paving the way for a more sustainable future in Mediterranean tourism.
In the first 18 months of implementation, the MAST project has successfully transitioned from foundational research to the creation of tangible outputs designed to support sustainability in the tourism sector. The main achievements of this reporting period centre on the development of the project’s core tools, underpinned by a robust transnational cooperation that has been vital to their quality and relevance.

The primary achievement has been the finalisation of the MAST Sustainability Protocol for SMEs in accommodation (D2.4.1). Led by UNIURB, this protocol serves as a practical guide for small and medium-sized hotels, translating the complex requirements of the ISO 21401:2018 standard into accessible, actionable steps. The protocol is grounded in a comprehensive comparative analysis (D2.3.1) that cross-referenced the standard's requirements with the real-world needs and barriers previously identified among accommodation providers across the partner countries.

Complementing the protocol, the partnership has developed the first FETA version of the AI-based self-assessment tool. This digital application, led by the technical experts at 3WIN, will allow accommodation providers to evaluate their sustainability performance and identify areas for improvement. The beta version is now ready for the crucial phase of user-based assessment, which will begin in the next reporting period.

The added value of the transnational cooperation has been clearly demonstrated in this phase. The project successfully combined UNIURB's academic rigour in developing the protocol with 3WIN's technical expertise in software development. This synergy was enriched by continuous feedback from all partners, ensuring the outputs are applicable across diverse national contexts in Spain, Slovenia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Greece. The 3rd Steering Committee Meeting and Stakeholder Info Day in Crete, involving over 40 local stakeholders, was a key moment for this collaboration, allowing for direct exchange and validation of the project's direction. Furthermore, effective project-wide dissemination, led by SMOC, has enhanced visibility through the production of two newsletters and a promotional video, actively shared by all partners.

fr.name: TOURism Innovative and Sustainable Management of flOws
fr.description: L'industrie du tourisme, dans les années pré-pandémiques, se concentrait presque exclusivement sur la croissance, sans se soucier des impacts à long terme sur les territoires. Ainsi, des défis persistants, comme le sur-tourisme, continuent d'affecter les destinations de la région méditerranéenne. TOURISMO reconnaît qu'une transition vers un tourisme conscient et durable nécessite des approches innovantes, la participation de divers acteurs, une amélioration de l'expérience touristique et un engagement plus fort des utilisateurs finaux. TOURISMO vise à relever ces défis en utilisant des technologies innovantes et de nouveaux services pour suivre et gérer les flux touristiques. En exploitant les bases posées par son prédécesseur HERIT-DATA, TOURISMO continuera à stimuler l'innovation grâce à de nouvelles méthodologies et outils dans les zones à forte pression touristique. Une meilleure gestion des flux sera mise en œuvre à travers le développement d'une structure de gestion de suivi & d'aide à la décision & des tests dans 7 sites pilotes MED. L'approche est basée sur l'utilisation de données provenant de divers ensembles de données et de technologies sur site (des caméras thermiques, des compteurs de passagers, des drones) pertinentes pour la présence, la densité, les trajectoires &les comportements des touristes,avec le support d'une version enrichie de la plateforme HERIT-DATA, développée et gérée par l'Université de Florence. Le partenariat intègre une variété de profils de localisation de 7 pays européens différentes parties prenantes du chaîne de valeur permettant de tester des solutions dans différents environnements, ainsi que de capitaliser les expériences antérieures, garantissant simultanément un haut degré d'innovation. Les actions prévues s'adressent directement ou indirectement aux principaux groupes de parties prenantes, à savoir les pouvoirs publics, les agences de tourisme, les OGD, les fournisseurs de solutions et les PME touristiques traditionnelles.
fr.expected_achievements:
WP1 aims at analysing the touristic habits and flows, revise the set of tourism indicators to be collected, gather complementary data and specify the pilot actions in the selected pilot areas. In parallel, HERIT-DATA platform will be fine tuned
WP2 aims at implementing the pilots, integrate collected data with  complementary data to make  tourism flows’ predictions and measure the impact of pilots. Additionally, WP 2 will deliver a framework on certifying  destinations as sustainable 
WP3 aims at communicating TOURISMO objectives and key findings to the target stakeholders through local workshops, facilitating further adoption of the tested solutions by developing use case scenaria and ensuring sustainability of the approach

en.name: TOURism Innovative and Sustainable Management of flOws
en.description:
The tourism industry, in the pre-pandemic years, focused almost exclusively on growth, with little concern for long-term impacts on territories. Thus, persistent challenges, like overtourism, continue to affect destinations & communities across the Med region.
TOURISMO acknowledges that a shift to conscious and sustainable tourism requires innovative approaches, participation of various ecosystem stakeholders, an improvement of the tourist experience & a stronger engagement of end users.
TOURISMO aims at addressing these challenges by using innovative technologies that offer new solutions and novel services to monitor and manage tourist flows.
By exploiting the foundations laid by its predecessor HERIT-DATA, TOURISMO will keep boosting innovation through new methodologies and tools in areas with high tourism pressure. 
Better flows handling will be implemented through the development of a monitoring & decision-making support management structure and testing in 8 MED pilot sites. The approach is based on utilizing data from various datasets and on-site technologies (ie thermal cameras, pax counters, drones, tags) relevant to tourists’ presence, density, trajectories and behaviors, 
with the support of an enriched version of HERIT-DATA platform, developed and managed by University of Florence. 
The partnership embeds a variety of location profiles (UNESCO areas, ports, city centers, beaches, cultural monuments, parks etc.) from 7 European countries (GR, IT, ES, CY, BG, HR & MT) & different stakeholders of the value chain (Development Agencies, RTOs, Scientific experts, Innovation Institutes and Intermediaries, NGOs) allowing to test solutions in different environments, as well as capitalizing previous experience, guaranteeing simultaneously a high degree of innovation.
Actions foreseen address directly or indirectly the key groups of stakeholders namely public authorities, tourist agencies, DMOs, solution providers &the traditional tourism SMEs.
en.expected_achievements:
WP1 aims at analysing the touristic habits and flows, revise the set of tourism indicators to be collected, gather complementary data and specify the pilot actions in the selected pilot areas. In parallel, HERIT-DATA platform will be fine tuned
WP2 aims at implementing the pilots, integrate collected data with  complementary data to make  tourism flows’ predictions and measure the impact of pilots. Additionally, WP 2 will deliver a framework on certifying  destinations as sustainable 
WP3 aims at communicating TOURISMO objectives and key findings to the target stakeholders through local workshops, facilitating further adoption of the tested solutions by developing use case scenaria and ensuring sustainability of the approach

As far as WP1 is concerned, A1.1, which focused on analyzing touristic flows and habits across the 8 pilot sites, has been successfully carried out. This involved conducting individual interviews with each pilot site to map stakeholders, identify spatial tourist flows and finally document these findings in D1.1. In addition, touristic material and data from the pilot sites has been collected in order to develop an initial state of the art, which will be crucial for the subsequent A1.5. To ensure consistent progress and collaboration, WP1 co-leaders FSMLR and FV have organized and co-led regular meetings with all partners to review WP1 advancements, discuss next steps, and collaborate on the development of Deliverables D1.1, D1.2, and the ongoing D1.5. A1.2 has also concluded, resulting in D.1.2 which gathers an enriched list of sets of indicators that are most relevant to the 8 pilot actions. FV developed a template within A1.3 for the collection, identification and mapping of complementary data in the 8 pilots, enabling the measurement of key, secondary and complementary indicators, as documented in Deliverable D1.2. Under A1.4, UNIFI is focused on fine-tuning the Herit-Data platform and has already started preliminary work in the set up of the digital twin platform based in HERIT-DATA solution for data ingestion and management of all the pilots.
In A1.5, the partners have already started to engage their local relevant stakeholders per pilot area through workshops in order to co-design the pilots through a participatory process, taking into account their needs and suggestions. FRI has prepared preliminary activities related to the common demonstration methodology that will be elaborated under D 1.5.1, to summon literature review, drafting of index, and collection of general information useful to fill in the different sections.

en.name: Testing Circular Economy solutions for the green transition of the Mediterranean area
en.description: CircleMED aims to convert the Euro-Med area into circular economy based territories, accelerating the diffusion of circular and climate-neutral practices and the green transition of the local civil, industrial and institutional systems. It points at reconciling our economies and human activities with the planetary boundaries. CircleMED’s integrated and systemic approach targets all the four helix stakeholders to promote the transition process to circular and sustainable production and consumption models, stimulating awareness and testing innovative circular solutions working closely with citizens, policy makers, business support organisations, academia, research entities and additional stakeholders, sharing knowledge and responsibilities for the green transition. Key elements CircleMED stress on are those considered fundamental for sustainable development: economic prosperity, environmental protection and social equity, balancing them to benefit current and future generations, targeting the three dimensions of citizens, SMEs and policy makers. CircleMED could be considered an evolutionary innovative project, testing solutions that already exist, but that have never been used together. It focuses on promoting circularity and testing resource efficiency particularly through an innovative circular assessment tool for SMEs, the creation of a transnational network of territorial circular hubs and the elaboration of plans for transferability of results in the whole Mediterranean area. The project aims at redesign, repair, reuse and recycle as driving scheme leading citizens, institutions and companies’ choices to a resource-efficient and competitive economy, further developing and scaling up circular approaches, methodologies and strategies easily adaptable to any kind of territory.
en.expected_achievements:
To support the active participation of citizens, understanding how they relate to circular, collaborative and sharing economy to build knowledge and methods for this, facilitating the circular transition and promoting socio-economic practices. 
To enhance circular innovative business solutions for the optimisation and implementation of circular economy approaches in local enterprises, enabling circular and climate neutral practices for transition.
To establish a permanent transnational cooperative system to strengthen the diffusion of Circular Economy in the MED area, promoting the diffusion of circular and climate-neutral practices and monitoring the sustainability of the transition process.
en.actual_achievements:
The kick-off meeting of CircleMED took place online on 5 February 2024. On this occasion the Steering Committee was formally established, administrative, financial management procedures and issues were reminded and the LP presented an overview of the project, highlighting the main activities, deliverables, outputs and upcoming steps.
In addition, a Interreg Euro-MED Innovative Sustainable Economy (ISE) Mission representative presented to the partnership the mission and activities of the programme, the Thematic Projects and the Mission Governance Projects.

In P1, the CircleMED partnership worked on refining the project’s internal organisation and management, as well as on developing initial activities for all WPs. 

In terms of communication, the CircleMED project adopted the official Interreg Euro-MED materials and logos, created its own brand identity, finalised its official website on the Interreg-EuroMED platform, and opened social media accounts on Instagram, Facebook and LinkedIn. 

WP1 aims to undergo analysis and develop tools to promote behavioural change and influence citizens' attitudes towards circular practices. 
During P1, the partnership worked on analysing the components of circular dynamics, identifying territories and circular domains.
The document 'Guidelines for case study identification and initial description', elaborated by the Activity 1.1 leader, provided the framework for identifying the boundaries and components of the local circular dynamics under study. 
The guidelines included:
- Objectives and main tasks of Activity 1.1 in WP1
- Identification of the area of interest
- Identification of the circular economy processes 
- Circularity matrix
- Reporting template
Each partner began to scan and identify the areas of interest within which circularity can and should be analysed, considering administrative relevance, citizen representativeness, and geographical scope (urban vs. rural). At the same time, they identified possible areas of circularity to be considered (e.g. organic waste, water, textile, technologic waste, packaging). 
Following the analysis, each partner submitted a short report describing: 1) the identification and initial description of the area of interest and 2) the definition of the relevant circular process matrix, identifying the relevant circular domains. The reports should be presented during the consortium meeting in Turin in July 2024.
During the analysis, the partners were asked to keep in mind and start “mapping” the stakeholders, which will be integral part of the Activity 1.2.

The mission of WP2 is to enhance circular innovative business solutions for the optimisation and implementation of circular economy approaches in local enterprises, enabling circular and climate-neutral practices for the transition. CircleMED intends to create a tool that facilitates the matching between SME/enterprise development needs and market requirements while offering an opportunity to test the validity and effectiveness of circular solutions. 
The first step of such a project being the analysis of currently available circularity tools. 
The partnership identified and selected Decision Support Tools (DTS): interactive software-based tools that support managers in making decisions at both the strategic and operational level, a structured approach designed to decision-making among stakeholders. 
The partners worked on a comprehensive list of DTS available (mainly, but not necessarily only in the field of circular economy). The tools proposed by each partner, will be analyzed and categorized by the entire partnership, according to specific parameters.
In vision of this task, the LP proposed an initial list of parameters for DTS analysis: "usability" (including “effectiveness”) indicators and "circular economy" criteria, to use as guidelines to analyse and categorise the DTS. The partners provided feedback on the preparatory document, to finalise the framework for the tools assessment, which will be carried out by all partners in P2.

The leader of the WP3 Activity 3.1 started working on carbon footprint reporting, by exploring the Interreg Euro-MED Carbon Footprint Calculator platform.
With regards to Activity 3.2, the partnership actively participated in the Thematic Community of the ISE Mission “Strengthening an innovative sustainable economy”, following the online communication and the Basecamp environment, and attending both the "2nd Innovation Camp of the ISE Mission", in Barcelona, on 28-29 May 2024, where the partners were actively involved in the activities of the event, sharing, exchanging and developing synergies between projects and stakeholders, and raising awareness of CircleMED, and the "Shaping Sustainable Innovation in the Mediterranean" event in Marseille, on 18-19 June 2024.
CircleMED focuses on the following objectives: 
-	to support the active participation of citizens and their understanding of the circular economy, to promote socio-economic practices and facilitate the transition; 
-	to improve the implementation and optimisation of circular practices for local businesses thanks to an online circularity assessment platform; 
-	and to work with local governments towards circular economy policies, while establishing a transnational system to promote the diffusion of circular economy practices in the MED region.

Regarding the first objective, the CircleMED partnership has already launched a citizens' survey to analyse people's awareness and attitudes towards circular economy and sustainability. The results will be collected at local level, with feedback from 7 different territories in the MED region. The analysis of the results will be the basis for the “Report on behavioural barriers for consumers and citizens”.
The survey was preceded by the identification of the target territories and the circular domains (the areas of interest within which circularity should be considered). Two "horizontal" (shared) circularity domains (organic waste and water) and one "vertical" (specific) domain per partner were selected.
Each partner analysed and defined the relevant stakeholders in their area to be involved in the Local Advisory Boards: citizens' representatives, public authorities and academia, business and trade unions, with the role of monitoring and providing guidance to ensure that the actions identified in the project move in the direction of supporting sustainable solutions. The definition and structuring of the Local Advisory Boards was based on a stakeholder analysis that identified, for each partner, the most important and influential actors in their area.

For the second objective of the project, a preliminary in-depth analysis of available online tools for circularity assessment was carried out by the partnership as a whole, in order to benchmark available solutions and identify areas for improvement and specifications for an upgraded / entirely new platform. The analysis resulted in a “Report on the assessment of tools for enterprises”.  The partnership joined forces to research and identify all relevant platforms on the market (mainly in English and other languages) and to carry out the analysis. The results were compared and weighted to ensure maximum professionalism of the final result.

With respect to the third objective, CircleMED Partnership actively participated in the activities of the Innovative Sustainable Economy Mission of Interreg-EuroMED, attending face-to-face and online meetings with the lead partner and other project partners, and actively engaged in activities and exchanges with the community. Other steps towards the achievement of the third objective, while based on the results and activities already in place, will be further developed in the next reporting Periods.

CircleMED carried out its carbon footprint reporting according to the Interreg-EuroMED guidelines and platform.

The CircleMED partnership met various times online and in-person. The official meetings have been:
- Kick-off meeting and Steering Committee – online, 5 February 2024
- CircleMED first operative meeting – online, 19th of March 2024
- Partnership meeting and Steering Committee – Turin, 17-18 July 2024
- Partnership meeting and Steering Committee – Seville, 3-4 December 2024.
On top of these, various online meetings were organised to ensure close collaboration and exchanges on the various activities: support was offered from the Activity Leaders to all partners with Q&A and explanatory session, feedback sessions made sure inputs from all partners were received and integrated at all stages and technical bilateral or collective online meetings made sure to tackle any technical aspect of the project, and ensure that the entire partnership was aligned and could contribute fully to the project.
WP1, focuses on supporting the active participation of citizens to circular economy.
In P3, the survey for citizens in the partners’ territories to analyse people's awareness and attitudes towards circular economy came to an end. The results have been analysed, resulting in the “Report on behavioural barriers for consumers and citizens”. The Deliverable was finalised by UNITO, supported by feedback by all Partners. 
The report integrated the responses, to explore shared trends and to assess potential space for improvement. The first section focuses on the core of the survey, dedicated to circular economy. The second section focuses on the part dedicated to global warming, providing essential contextual insights. From the outcomes of both sections, the conclusions are drawn. A section of the document is dedicated to a comparison across the target regions.
The report assesses how the behaviour most associated with circularity among the respondents is related to recycling, self-reported as the most practiced action. Other actions (reuse, repair, reduction), are reported as much less common, indicating an overall rather vague awareness of the fundamentals of circular economy.
The main barriers in the adoption of circular economy behaviours lie in resistance to behavioural change and limited availability and high cost of sustainable products. Respondents do not perceive the absence of regulations as major obstacle, and view policy interventions targeting production processes and economic incentives as the most effective measure to accelerate the transition to a circular economy. 
The results of the survey are one of the documents and tools of discussion with the Local Advisory Board (LAB). The results of the LABs will be integrated in the “Guidelines for local public support and citizens’ participation“ and in the “Capitalisation plan for circular sustainability”.
In P3, the selection and contact of stakeholders to be involved in the LAB was finalised, under the coordination of SERDA, with a presentation during the Partnership Meeting in Sarajevo, 1 April 2025. Meetings of the LABs have already taken place (either in-person or online).
Building from the findings of the survey and meeting of the LAB, some partners already started organising Future Labs: animation initiatives targeting citizens, to enhance societal dialogue. The Partners decided to structure the events as “gamification” workshops. UNITO organised a session on the benefits of gamification for engagement and information retention. Various board games were proposed. Finally, due to variabilities in the target groups and language constraint, it was decided that each partner assess the best board game to be used for its activity.
The activities serve as base for the “Guidelines for local public support and citizens’ participation”, and for discussion for the LAB, to assess to what extent local policies fit-in at citizens level.

WP2 aims at enhancing innovative solutions for the optimisation and implementation of circular economy in local enterprises.
In P3, the “Report on the assessment of tools for enterprises” published in P2 was used as basis for the definition of the technical specification of the CircleMED circularity self-assessment platform. The characteristics, discussed by the partnership, were finally defined: 
-	after evaluation of multiple “access profiles”, it was decided to create one only access type to streamline the process 
-	The tool will collect information on the company via a questionnaire
-	Three options of “depth of analysis”with different length and detail of questionnaire will allow a flexible approach
-	The tool will provide respondents (businesses) with a benchmark and gap analysis with respect to pre-defined values (e.g. ISO 59020, ISO 59010), a report,and an Interactive Action Plan
-	The tool will integrate an Interactive User Portfolio, for companies to monitor their evolution in time.
A tender was launched by CCI du VAR, to identify a suitable provider for the technical development.
WP3 focuses on raising awareness on framework support for territorial deployment of circular economy principles, creating dialogue and synergies between stakeholders, and building political support.
In P3, the Partners led by ANETEL developed a self-assessment framework based on the OECD Checklist for Action “The Circular Economy in Cities and Regions”. Using the tool as a base, with the support of the LAB, each partner will anayse regulatory obstacles and drivers in its local, regional or national policy instruments.
In P3 CircleMED Partnership actively participated in the activities of the ISE Mission:
-	calls on the Mentoring and Twinning programme and C4I/DV - peer review process
-	ISE Mission CoP in Sarajevo, 2-3 April 2025
-	“technical” meetings of the ISE Mission (e.g. communication)
-	 2nd International Workshop of the ProcuraMED Project, 20 May 2025.
CircleMED partners keep monitoring its Carbon Footprint, to be reported in P4.

fr.description: CircleMED vise à faire évoluer la zone Med en territoires basés sur l'économie circulaire, accélérant la diffusion de pratiques circulaires et climatiquement neutres et la transition verte des systèmes civils, industriels et institutionnels locaux. Il s'agit de réconcilier nos économies et nos activités humaines avec les limites planétaires. L'approche intégrée et systémique de CircleMED cible la quadruple hélix pour promouvoir la transition vers des modèles de production et de consommation circulaires et durables, travaillant en étroite collaboration avec citoyens, décideurs politiques, organisations professionnelles, universités, entités d’éducation et recherche et d'autres parties prenantes afin de sensibiliser et de tester des solutions innovantes, partageant les connaissances et les responsabilités pour la transition verte. Les éléments clés sur lesquels mettre l'accent sont ceux qui garantissent le développement durable : prospérité économique, protection de l'environnement et équité sociale, en les équilibrant au profit des générations actuelles et futures, s’adressant aux citoyens, aux PME et aux décideurs politiques. CircleMED peut être considéré un projet d’innovation évolutive, qui teste des solutions qui existent déjà, mais qui n'ont jamais été utilisées cumulativement. Il promeut la circularité et teste l'efficacité des ressources, particulièrement grâce à un nouvel outil d'évaluation de la circularité destiné aux PME, à la création d'un réseau transnational de pôles circulaires territoriaux et à l'élaboration de plans de transférabilité des résultats dans la zone MED. Le projet vise à remodeler, réparer, réutiliser et recycler pour développer un schéma directeur conduisant les choix des citoyens, des institutions et des entreprises vers une économie efficace en termes de ressources et compétitive, en développant et en mettant à l'échelle des approches, des méthodologies et des stratégies circulaires facilement adaptables à tout type de territoire.

fr.name: HERItage and territory resilience through sustainable Tourism, climate change ADAPtation and ciTizen engagement
fr.description: Le tourisme est une industrie clé pour les régions Euro-MED. Le tourisme patrimonial participe fortement au produit touristique global. Pourtant, l'activité humaine & le changement climatique présentent des risques pour les biens du patrimoine culturel (PC) & du patrimoine naturel (PN) dans les milieux ruraux et urbains. Les impacts sur le patrimoine et le tourisme n'ont pas encore été examinés et traités de manière adéquate. Ceci génère des pressions socio-économiques supplémentaires aux destinations, qui subissent déjà les conséquences du COVID19. Afin d'améliorer la durabilité et la résilience du secteur, il est nécessaire d'accroître les capacités d'innovation, d'adaptation et d'atténuation de l’impact, tout en garantissant la qualité et la protection du PC et PN. Des plans d'action et des stratégies appropriés seront à élaborér pour relever les défis actuels, et libérer le potentiel inexploité des PC et PN, vers la neutralité climatique. HERIT ADAPT encouragera la coopération entre les principales parties prenantes en constituant des Groupes de Travail Territoriaux interdisciplinaires. Ils permettront de coconstruire le Modèle de Tourisme Durable HERIT ADAPT (MTD). Ce MTD, basé sur des données, sera testé dans 8 Pilotes présentant différentes typologies, dont des sites UNESCO et des monuments moins connus dans les zones côtières, d'arrière-pays & montagneuses. Le MTD tiendra compte des besoins et des visions territoriales, des cadres existants, et de l'offre technologique globale. Ceci permettra d'utiliser les outils et les solutions existants et de valoriser les connaissances transnationales acquises dans le cadre d'autres projets. HERIT ADAPT impliquera de manière efficiente les groupes cibles pour identifier les leçons apprises et développer ensemble des stratégies de tourisme durable et résilient. Enfin, il transformera les défis en opportunités en utilisant le PC et PN comme un vecteur pour communiquer les atouts dans le contexte du changement climatique.
fr.expected_achievements:
Development of the methodology of risk monitoring and management leading to co-created HERIT ADAPT Sustainable Tourism Model which will facilitate protection of natural and cultural assets and reinforce destinations’ resilience. 
Pilot testing of HERIT ADAPT Sustainable Tourism Model in 8 use cases of different typologies combining monitoring and management of risks, and related protection of natural and cultural heritage, being assets at the center of tourism development
Enhanced Tourism Sustainability in the partner territories and beyond driven by lessons learnt from the HERIT ADAPT Sustainable Tourism Model testing and relevant knowledge transfer

en.name: HERItage and territory resilience through sustainable Tourism, climate change ADAPtation and ciTizen engagement
en.description:
Tourism represents an important industry for Euro-MED with Heritage tourism being a significant element of the overall tourist product. Yet, human activity and climate change pose risks to both cultural heritage (CH) and natural heritage (NH) assets in rural and urban environments. Impacts on heritage and subsequently to tourism have not yet been adequately examined and dealt with, posing additional socio-economic pressures to destinations that already suffer covid-19 consequences. In order to enhance sustainability and resilience of the sector, increased innovation, adaptation and mitigation capacities are needed, in line with quality principles to ensure the CH and NH protection. Proper action plans and strategies need to be developed or improved to address current challenges on one hand and unleash the untapped potential of CH and NH towards climate neutrality on the other. At the same time, sectoral fragmentation needs to be tackled to enable effective decision making.

HERIT ADAPT will foster cooperation among key stakeholders by activating interdisciplinary Territorial Working Groups (TWGs) to co-design the data-driven HERIT ADAPT Sustainable Tourism Model (STM) that will be tested in 8 pilot Euro-MED regions of different typologies, including UNESCO sites and lesser known monuments in coastal, hinterland and mountainous areas. The STM will take into account territorial needs and visions, existing frameworks and plans and the global technology offer, enabling the use of existing tools and solutions and the valorization of transnational knowledge gained from other projects. Following a co-creation approach HERIT ADAPT will truly engage target groups to identify lessons learnt and co-develop Sustainable and Resilient tourism Strategies. Finally, it will turn challenges into opportunities by using heritage as a vehicle for communicating CH and NH assets in the context of climate change and inspire the uptake of HERIT ADAPT innovative solutions from other regions.
en.expected_achievements:
Development of the methodology of risk monitoring and management leading to co-created HERIT ADAPT Sustainable Tourism Model which will facilitate protection of natural and cultural assets and reinforce destinations’ resilience.
Pilot testing of HERIT ADAPT Sustainable Tourism Model in 8 use cases of different typologies combining monitoring and management of risks, and related protection of natural and cultural heritage, being assets at the center of tourism development
Enhanced Tourism Sustainability in the partner territories and beyond driven by lessons learnt from the HERIT ADAPT Sustainable Tourism Model testing and relevant knowledge transfer

-	All partners participated in Communication Online Meeting (November 19, 2024), focused on improving communication strategies and promoting project results through social media and conferences.
-	PP2 ATHENA contributed to all activities of WP1 and led activity 1.3. This included preparing templates and questionnaires for pilot testing, as well as compiling a report mapping the technological state of the art in relation to pilot testing.

en.name: Supporting Adaptation to Mediterranean Sea Level Rise
en.description:
Climate change is evident in many aspects, among them there is the progressive increase of the average sea level, which is affecting the rare extremes too, both on their frequency and intensity.

The assessment of risks deriving from sea level variations requires a robust quantification of all the elements bringing the sea level to become a hazard thus, for an effective adaptation to future sea conditions, it is mandatory to have reliable knowledge on scenarios for sea level trends.

This is the challenge addressed by this project with the objective of improving the assessment of risks deriving from the Mediterranean sea level variations, through a robust quantification of its hazardous source.

Mediterranean coastal impacts are deduced from global sea level trends or climate simulation each affected by significant uncertainties, furthermore, there is an open debate, in the scientific community, on them. As a consequence, there is an urgent need to quantify the likelihood of available scientific information on the expected XXI century sea level trends, in the Mediterranean sub-basins, with an estimate of the uncertainties.

This project outputs are expected to contribute in meeting this need, defining best practices on sea level trends usage, in the risk assessment processes, according to the sensitivity of impacts to the hazard information likelihood. Best practices, supported by examples of applications and summary datasets, will help stakeholders to flow the scientific information into the risk assessment logic. Benefits are expected to reach a wide spectra of stakeholders from the economic and safety to the coastal ecosystem preservation sectors.

The project transnational partnership brings the inter-regional portrait of the challenge together with the interdisciplinarity required to understand how anthropic solutions and better preservation of the coastal ecosystems have to be involved in resilience and adaptation actions to the progressive sea level rise.
en.expected_achievements:
The objective is to identify and collect the information required to analyse the risks related to sea level rise, according to the impacts which are mostly felt by stakeholders, and to harvest data of available simulations on future sea level trends.
The objective is to evaluate the likelihood of Mediterranean sea level trends scenarios, to develop a methodology for a proper use of such data in the risk assessment processes and to provide guidelines and benchmarks for the methodology application.
The objective is to validate the methodology on the sea level trends data usage in risk evaluation processes, by means of examples of its application, which have been identified thanks to the stakeholders participation and the experts analyses.
en.actual_achievements:
The 1st semester was mainly a preparatory period for the engagement of local stakeholders, setting up the management & administrative procedures, and elaboration of the main project outputs/deliverables/studies of the next phases of the project. Thus, the main progress can be summarized bellow:
Regarding Administrative and Management issues, PPs:
-Finalized the pre-contracting clarifications requested by the Programme Officers
-Signed the Partnership Agreement and Subsidy Contract
-Prepared the Rules of Procedure of the Steering Committee
-Prepared the Monitoring & Evaluation Plan, setting internal procedures for monitoring progress and ensuring quality of cooperation and outputs
-Allocated roles between the staff that will participate in the projects’ implementation and appointed representatives from each organization that will participate in the Steering Committee and Technical Committee
-Set up a shared online space (Google Drive) for collaboratively working on Deliverables
-Actively used Basecamp for monitoring deadlines and storing final deliverables
-Organized the pre Kick-Off meeting (online) on 20.12.2023 with all partners participation
-Organized the Kick-Off meeting (physical) on 20.2.2024 in Thessaloniki, Greece with all partners participation
-Organized the SC online meeting (online) on 12.4.2024, attended by all partners
-Organized the 1st Semester Quality Check related to internal collaboration between the partnership.

Regarding Communication and Dissemination, the Partnership:
-Finalized the Communication Strategy
-Created the project social media (LinkedIN, Instagram, X)
-Created the project website according to the guidelines of the Programme
-Created the 1st Newsletter of the project which was distributed to local stakeholders by the partners social media, individual contacts and websites
-Dedicated a specific section in their institutional website about the MedSeaRise project
-Printed and located the project poster in visible place in their premises
-Had an active presence in the social media (Instagram: 40 Followers, 18 posts, 804 profiles reached) (LinkedIN: 108 Followers, 18 posts, 74 connections, 10.578 total post impressions, 1.987 total unique views) (X: 12 Followers)
-Had external dissemination from local online media press (3 in Greece, 3 in Malta), and gave a video interview about the expected results of MedSeaRise in Maltese online media.

Regarding contribution to the Amplification Strategy, the Partnership:
-Organized an online meeting with representatives of the Natural Heritage Mission TCP and IDP on 17.4.2024 (LP1, PP2) to present the main aims of MedSeaRise and coordinate, identify and plan the projects’ Amplification Strategy
-Attended the TCP and IDP common meeting in Rovinj, Croatia on 26,27/6/2024
-Participate in the Natural Heritage Mission Working Groups
-Drafted the projects’ Amplification Strategy that is planned to be delivered in the frame of the A1.3.

Regarding the implementation of the Work Packages, namely WP1, a summary is presented bellow:
-Activity 1.1. PP2 started the survey of scientific paper on sea level projection for the Mediterranean area. The survey is in progress in strict collaboration with PP6. This contributes to the deliverable D.1.1.1. Numerical data of future scenarios have been collected from CMIP6 and MEDCORDEX sets of simulations. Besides sea level, air temperature and precipitation are available for at least three scenarios, namely RCP2.6, RCP4.5 and RCP8.5. For sea level, in addition the RCP7.0 is available. Time extension of the scenarios spans from the historical section, which is covering the second half of XX century, and the projections, that go to the end of the XXI century. This contributes to the deliverable D.1.1.2.
-Activity 1.2. PP2 defined the methodology for stakeholder engagement and information harvesting. The implementation of the methodology started and the survey of potential stakeholders completed. Future climate scenarios information, in graphical format, have been prepared and let available to support the interactions with stakeholders. The stakeholder survey and engagement will be done during the next period. This contributes to the deliverables D.1.2.1, D1.2.2 and D.1.2.3.
-Activity 1.3. The deliverable D.1.3.1 “Definition of project contribution to Amplification Strategy” that was issued at the end of this project period, is finalized and uploaded on Basecamp. 

MedSeaRise showcase a great example of combination of organizations with different expertise to achieve the project goals. The scientific results of the 1st Semester have been collaboratively prepared by the scientific PPs (PP2, PP5) and were thoroughly presented & explained to the partnership to be easily transferred to potential local and international stakeholders. This excellent collaboration during the 1st semester can be also seen in the responses of the 1st quality check, highlighting the value of cooperation for the participating organizations.
During the second project period, MedSeaRise achieved significant progress across coordination, management, communication, and research activities. ANATOLIKI played a central role in project coordination, preparing the carbon footprint report with contributions from all partners and developing a quality evaluation form based on the Monitoring & Evaluation Plan. This feedback mechanism helped identify areas for improvement in collaboration. Additionally, ANATOLIKI prepared the first progress report and facilitated the consolidation of individual reports from all partners, ensuring smooth communication with the Joint Secretariat on administrative, financial, and reporting matters.

MedSeaRise maintained an active presence in communication and dissemination efforts. Social media engagement remained a priority, with different partners managing content each month and the Lead Partner (LP) overseeing publications. The project recorded steady growth across platforms, reaching significant audiences.(LinkedIN: 147 Followers, 37 posts(in total), 92 connections, 17.004 total post impressions,2,963 Members reached ) (X: 12 Followers). The second newsletter(https://medsearise.interreg-euro-med.eu/wp-content/uploads/sites/79/medsearise-newsletter-final.pdf), published in December, featured contributions from all partners. MedSeaRise also actively participated in key international events. At the 3rd International Conference on Adriatic Biodiversity Protection (ADRIBIOPRO 2024) in Kotor, Montenegro, a dedicated workshop on sea level rise was organized, bringing together 44 experts to discuss climate change scenarios, marine indicators, river discharge impacts, and ecosystem responses. These discussions informed the project’s methodological developments. Additionally, a project meeting at the Institute of Marine Biology focused on stakeholder engagement, case studies, and effective communication of scientific findings. MedSeaRise also contributed to an Interreg Euro-MED webinar on the EU Nature Restoration Law and participated in the Natural Heritage Mission’s Joint Communication Meeting, enhancing collaboration and project visibility.

Across work packages, partners advanced research and stakeholder engagement. WP1 activities included risk analysis, scientific data collection, and stakeholder awareness efforts. PP2 led the development of impact indexes for risk assessments and supported case study selection. MedSeaRise partners strengthened ties with governance projects like Community4Nature, while the Euro-MED Academy was identified as a potential platform for peer-reviewed project outputs. Case study development progressed with refined methodologies for integrating stakeholder input. Efforts were made to address technical challenges, including the collection of additional sea level data for risk assessments. Meetings with organizations such as Cerema and CASA explored stormwater management solutions, and stakeholder engagement was intensified through targeted invitations and discussions.

Partners actively contributed to institutional dialogues and capacity-building efforts. PP4 and PP6 participated in the second Mission for Natural Heritage Institutional Dialogue in Málaga, showcasing MedSeaRise’s role in sustainable adaptation strategies. An online meeting in December between PP2, PP4, and PP6 focused on risk analyses, statistical indexes, and methodologies for assessing sea level rise impacts. The team also generated tide-level datasets using the TPXO10v2a model to support future stakeholder meetings. Throughout this period, MedSeaRise remained committed to advancing scientific understanding, stakeholder engagement, and practical applications for climate adaptation.

Finally the deliverables D.1.1.1 and D.1.3.1. were finalized and released. 
ANATOLIKI played a central role in project coordination, preparing the Carbon Footprint Report with input from all partners and designing a Quality Evaluation Form for this semester, whose feedback highlighted some areas for improvement.  

The dissemination of the project was managed on a rotating basis by different partners, with the Lead Partner overseeing overall publication. The project’s visibility grew steadily across platforms, reaching substantial audiences (LinkedIn: 189 followers, 49 posts, 92 connections, 12,115 post impressions, and 2,173 members reached; Instagram: 49 posts, 100 followers, 39 accounts reached; X: 14 followers). In early July, the project published its third newsletter on the project's website and was disseminated by each partners to their stakeholders. MedSeaRise was represented by two representatives from the Lead Partner in the Natural Heritage Mission's Community of Practice meeting, held on July 2–3 at the National Technical University of Athens (NTUA). During the European Maritime Day workshops, the project was presented to the participants, drawing strong interest from attendees. MedSeaRise also contributed actively to the communication-focused sessions of the #SolveTheChallenge 2025 NHM 's campaign. The preparation for this meeting was organised by the LP and PP2.

On April 1st and 2nd, a project meeting was held in Malta with the participation of all partners. The main focus of the meeting was the discussion of each partner's case study and the activities related to their WP1 commitments.

PP2 successfully coordinated WP1 activities, ensuring that all partners met their commitments and deadlines. They monitored progress, guided the preparation of deliverables, and kept the Lead Partner informed. PP2 organized scientific data and future sea level rise scenarios, consolidated stakeholder-driven impact assessments, and summarized case studies prepared by each partner. PP2 has actively contributed to the Community4Nature Amplification Room, supporting the wider dissemination of project results.

PP3, leading Work Package 3, initiated the organization of upcoming activities in order to validate the Medsearise methodology. PP4 undertook several dissemination actions, including the organization of Info Days, and also supported other partners in WP1 and WP2, while finalizing the deliverables of WP2. Additionally, PP4 and PP5 contributed to dissemination efforts through presentations of the project’s expected results.

All partners successfully completed their case studies during this semester, fully achieving their objectives, finalizing the respective deliverables within each Work Package, and establishing the basis for the subsequent project activities.

fr.name: Supporting Adaptation to Mediterranean Sea Level Rise
fr.description: Le changement climatique a des conséquences directes, dont l’augmentation du niveau moyen de la mer, qui entraîne l’apparition d’événements climatiques extrêmes, tant en termes de fréquence que d’intensité. L’évaluation des risques découlant des variations du niveau de la mer nécessite une quantification des éléments qui font que le niveau de la mer devient un danger pour une adaptation efficace aux conséquences futures de la montée des eaux. Il est indispensable de disposer de connaissances fiables sur les scénarios des évolutions du niveau de la mer. L’objectif de MedSeaRise est d’améliorer l’évaluation des risques découlant des variations du niveau de la mer, grâce à une quantification robuste des risques de montée des eaux. Les impacts côtiers méditerranéens sont évalués à partir des tendances globales de variation du niveau de la mer ou de simulation de conséquences du changement climatique. Il est urgent de quantifier la probabilité des informations sur les tendances attendues du niveau de la mer au XXIe siècle, avec une estimation la part d’incertitudes. MedSeaRise entend répondre à ce besoin, en définissant des pratiques exemplaires sur l’utilisation des données sur le niveau de la mer, dans les processus d’évaluation des risques, en fonction de la sensibilité des impacts et de la probabilité des scénarios. Des études de cas aideront les intervenants à intégrer l’information scientifique dans une logique d’évaluation des risques. Les résultats de MedSeaRise s’adresseront à un large éventail d’intervenants, des secteurs de l’économie et de la sécurité jusqu’aux secteurs de la préservation des écosystèmes côtiers en passant par les pouvoirs publics. MedSeaRise est un partenariat transnational qui repose sur une approche interdisciplinaire pour comprendre comment les solutions anthropiques et une meilleure préservation des écosystèmes côtiers peuvent contribuer aux actions de résilience et d’adaptation face à l’élévation progressive du niveau de la mer.
